title.zh: 柢山上冬死夏生的陵居怪鱼
title.en: Hill-Dwelling Fish That Dies in Winter and Lives in Summer

summary.zh: 柢山怪鱼，形状如牛却住在山坡上，蛇尾有翼，翅膀生在肋下，冬天蛰伏如死、夏天复生；吃它的肉不生痈肿。
summary.en: A fish of Mount Di shaped like an ox yet living on hillsides, with a serpent's tail and wings sprouting beneath its ribs; it dies each winter and revives each summer, and eating it prevents tumorous swellings.

description.zh:
柢山多水，不生草木。《南山经》记："有鱼焉，其状如牛，陵居，蛇尾有翼，其羽在魼下，其音如留牛，其名曰鯥，冬死而夏生，食之无肿疾。"经文明明称它为鱼，它却长着牛的身形，住在山坡上（陵居），拖一条蛇尾，肋下（魼，旧注读为胁）生出翅膀，叫声如牛鸣。一身之上，鱼、牛、蛇、鸟四类的零件拼在一起，是《山海经》拼合式造物的标本。

最奇的是"冬死而夏生"：它冬天死去，夏天又活过来。郭璞注以蛰伏解之——冬蛰如死，非真死也，这大约是古人对冬眠现象最直白的记录之一。在巫术的对应逻辑里，一头能从死里回来的兽，肉自然克制坏死之症，所以"食之无肿疾"，吃它可免痈肿。历代图本画鯥，多作一头肋生双翅、蛇尾拖地的牛形鱼卧在山坡上，鳞、翼、蹄俱全，是图卷里构造最繁复的水族。
description.en:
Mount Di is rich in water and bare of plants, and its one marvel is emphatically called a fish while resembling almost anything else. The lu has the body of an ox; it dwells on the hillside, not in the streams; a serpent's tail trails behind it, and wings sprout from beneath its ribs — the character qu being read by the old commentators as the flanks. Its call is the lowing of an ox. Fish, ox, snake, and bird are riveted into one body, a textbook specimen of the Shanhaijing's composite creatures.

Stranger still is the clause 'it dies in winter and lives in summer'. Guo Pu's commentary resolves the paradox as hibernation — a winter torpor like death rather than death itself — making this one of the plainest early Chinese records of the phenomenon. Sympathetic magic supplies the medical rider: a beast that returns from death must master dead and rotting flesh, so eating it protects against tumorous swellings. Illustrated editions render the lu as a winged, scaled ox-fish couched on a slope, tail coiled and hooves showing, the most elaborately assembled water creature in the scrolls.

value.en: Dies in winter, revives in summer

sources.en: Guo Pu, commentary on the Shanhaijing
